title: Filter Metrics by Line of Business and Topic
story: As a data analyst, I want to filter the Metrics Glossary table by Line of Business and Topic categories, so that I can quickly narrow down to the metrics relevant to my area of focus.
description:
Adds dynamic taxonomy-based filters to the Metrics Glossary data table. Users can filter the table by Line of Business, Topic, or any other tenant-configured taxonomy. Filters are generated dynamically based on the tenant's taxonomy configuration — adding new taxonomies automatically adds corresponding filter controls.

The implementation ensures scalability: filter UI elements are built from the taxonomy definition rather than hardcoded, so future taxonomy types are supported without code changes.
acceptance_criteria: Filter controls for Line of Business and Topic appear above or alongside the Metrics Glossary table
Selecting a filter value narrows the table to metrics matching that taxonomy value
Filters are generated dynamically based on the tenant's configured taxonomies
Adding a new taxonomy type automatically adds a corresponding filter control
Clearing a filter restores the full metric list
Multiple filters can be applied simultaneously
ux_changes: Dynamic filter dropdowns above the Metrics Glossary table for each tenant taxonomy (Line of Business, Topic, etc.). Filters narrow the table in real-time.
middleware_changes: Filter query logic joins metrics with taxonomy assignments and applies filter criteria. Dynamically builds filter options from the tenant's taxonomy value list.
api_changes: Metrics list endpoint accepts taxonomy filter parameters to return filtered results.
implementation_notes: Filters are built dynamically from the taxonomy definitions rather than being hardcoded. This means adding a new taxonomy for a customer automatically adds a new filter without code changes.
comment_summary: Developer noted the extra time needed for dynamic filter generation based on per-customer taxonomies. Pushed to staging for review.
